name: Khachapuri
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast and 1 tsp sugar in 1/4 cup warm water. Let stand for 5-10 minutes until foamy.
2. In a large bowl, mix milk, remaining 2 tablespoons sugar, butter, salt, and flour to form a soft dough. Turn the dough to coat all sides. Cover and let rise until doubled in bulk.
3. Shred cheeses and combine with filling ingredients. Refrigerate.
4. Grease a 9-inch springform pan well.
5. Punch down the dough, shape into a ball, and roll out to a 20-inch circle. Place the dough (folded in half) into the prepared pan, letting excess hang over the edge.
6. Mound cheese filling onto the dough, then fold dough up over the filling to create 8-12 evenly spaced pleats. Twist and pinch edges together to form a rough knob.
7. Cover with a buttered 12-inch square of wax paper. Let rise until doubled in bulk.
8. Brush loaf with glaze made from 1 egg white and 1 tablespoon water (or use margarine). Bake at 375°F for 40-50 minutes, or until deeply golden brown.
9. Remove bread from the pan and place directly on the oven rack. Continue baking for an additional 5 minutes to brown the bottom crust.
10. Cool on a rack for 45 minutes before cutting into wedges.
Served with soup or salad, this is a full meal. You can substitute 4 ounces muenster cheese and 4 ounces Swiss cheese for 8 ounces cheddar cheese.
